name: Greek Stuffed Zucchini
recipeInstructions:
1. Either core the zucchinis or split them lengthwise and scoop out the innards. Chop this up and set aside.
2. Finely chop onion and celery. In a pot, combine onion, celery, olive oil, rice, water, pepper, and salt. Cook over medium heat for 25 minutes.
3. Meanwhile, mix the juice of 2 lemons with egg whites in a bowl. Mix the juice of the third lemon with yolks in another bowl; refrigerate.
4. After 25 minutes, add the chopped zucchini to the pot. Cook an additional 5 minutes to soften the zucchini. Preheat oven to 180°C (350°F). Place zucchini shells in a baking dish.
5. Remove mixture from heat. Stir in parsley, bread crumbs, and egg white mixture. Fill cored zucchinis or zucchini "boats" in the baking dish.
6. Bake for 40 minutes, covered.
7. Take yolk mixture from the fridge. Add any remaining juice from the pans to the yolk mixture while stirring. Spoon this over the stuffed zucchini and return to oven for an additional 5 minutes.
Author's Notes:
This recipe was tried last night (from Laurel's Kitchen). Since some ingredients were not available, carrots substituted for celery and pearled barley replaced the rice. The dish turned out well. For more zucchini recipes, consider purchasing Laurel's Kitchen. Difficulty: easy. Precision: measure ingredients.
